title: centrolepis strigosa var. patersonii
intro: Centrolepis strigosa var. patersonii, also known as the New Zealand parsley fern or Patterson's centrolepis, is a rare and endangered species of fern that belongs to the Centrolepidaceae family. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Centrolepis strigosa var. patersonii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas or infusions, although its use is highly regulated due to its endangered status.
